title: e2e: default to "podman version" in is-server-up tests
body:
Every e2e test starts its own podman server. And every one of those tests runs 'podman info' to check that the server is up and running. This makes test logs painful to read, because you click a link, get taken to the test name, then have to scroll down and down past 170+ lines to get to the useful part of the log.

Workaround: run '...info --format SOMETHING', so we just get
one short line of output. The SOMETHING is irrelevant, none
of it is actually interesting for default testing.
 
As a special case, on tests that run RestartRemoteService()
(containers.conf and network config changes), display the full
'podman info' output. This may prove useful some day. It also
makes it very obvious to even the most casual reader that
something different is happening.
 
Also, reduce retry timeout (2s -> .25s). The retry doesn't
seem to trigger often, but when it does, 2s is excessive.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
none
```

comments.body:
Tested on my hammer-CI PR. Sample results:
* link to [a containers.conf result, with both podman-version and podman-info](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4591371554652160/html/int-remote-fedora-38-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Verify-podman-containers-conf-usage-sysctl-test--1)
* link to [pretty much all the other results](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4591371554652160/html/int-remote-fedora-38-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-logs-using-container-with-container-log-size--journald--1)

See how easy it is to click on the second one and immediately know what went wrong? (Assuming you're following the missing-logs flake)

Welp, looks like [we need delayForService()](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6339312967483392/html/int-remote-debian-12-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-ns-podman-run-cgroup-private-test--1). ([Two flakes](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6480050455838720/html/int-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-wait-podman-wait-on-a-stopped-container--1)). Or some other mechanism.

> Welp, looks like [we need delayForService()](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6339312967483392/html/int-remote-debian-12-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-run-ns-podman-run-cgroup-private-test--1). ([Two flakes](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6480050455838720/html/int-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-wait-podman-wait-on-a-stopped-container--1)). Or some other mechanism.

Ok thanks for checking, I am fine to merge this but the hang in the debian tests is concerning: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6658565889327104
Given the number of retries there and that it hangs in every single one of them this seems like a hard failure for some reason. Or at the very least it will flake to hell if it passes eventually so I am not going to rerun. I do not see anythnig obvious in the logs, very strange.
The debian hang is #19010. It's failing in all PRs, although it does seem to be failing harder than usual here.

Can you change the retry interval from 2 seconds two like 500 ms or less? It feels wrong to wait so long if we miss the first connection. Maybe podman-remote version is faster then info thus misses the first connection and has to wait 2s.

With around 1800 remote tests that is at least 60 min wasted wait time.
see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19182 for a (hopefully) faster way to check for the socket
